title: BAI Implements 3x15 Scoring System for Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: This change by BAI will impact all junior domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in India.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the implementation of a new 3x15 scoring system for U-13, U-15, and U-17 domestic ranking tournaments and national championships, set to debut in Ernakulam.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes as Runner-Up at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open, showcasing strong form on the international stage.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth's impressive run at the US Open concluded with a loss in the final to Su Li Yang, where he finished as the runner-up.

title: Rounak Chouhan Upsets World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen at US Open
indian_angle: Young Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a significant upset by defeating a top-ranked player at the US Open.
summary: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan, a self-proclaimed Cristiano Ronaldo fan, caused a major upset at the US Open Super 300 by defeating World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen.

title: BAI Updates Selection Criteria for Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships
indian_angle: This update from BAI directly affects aspiring young Indian shuttlers aiming to represent the country at the Asian Junior Championships.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued a further addendum to the selection criteria for the upcoming Badminton Asia U17 & U15 Junior Championships 2026.

title: Chennai to Host Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Tournament
indian_angle: This tournament is a key domestic event for senior Indian badminton players to improve their national rankings.
summary: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026 is scheduled to take place in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, from July 16th to 23rd.

title: Thomas Cup Squad Members Reassemble for Asian Games
indian_angle: Key Indian players, including the return of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand, are preparing for the upcoming Asian Games.
summary: Members of India's Thomas Cup winning squad are reassembling to prepare for the Asian Games, with the notable return of the women's doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.
